sentiment.rationale: A sweeping pronouncement of cosmic judgment — God will consume everything from the face of the land, targeting Judah's idolaters and complacent.
Calls for repentance before the day of wrath; judgment upon Philistia, Moab, Ammon, Ethiopia, and Assyria.
Opens with woe against Jerusalem's rebellious leaders but transforms into joyous restoration — God will rejoice over His people with singing.

missingCharacters.name: Zephaniah
missingCharacters.description: A prophet of royal lineage who prophesied during the reign of Josiah against Judah's idolatry and foretold the great day of the Lord.

summaries.summary: God declares He will utterly consume everything from the land; judgment falls upon Judah for Baal worship, complacency, and those who say God will do neither good nor evil.
Zephaniah calls the meek to seek the Lord before the day of anger; judgment is pronounced upon Philistia, Moab, Ammon, Ethiopia, and proud Assyria.
Woe upon polluted Jerusalem whose leaders are corrupt; but God will purify the nations, gather the afflicted remnant, and rejoice over them with singing.

crossReferences.note: 'The day of the Lord is at hand' — shared imagery of the approaching day of the Lord.
Seek the Lord and ye may be hid in the day of the Lord's anger — echoes Amos's call to seek and live.
God rejoicing over His people parallels Isaiah's imagery of the bridegroom rejoicing over the bride.

verses.reason: 'The great day of the Lord is near' — one of the most vivid day-of-the-Lord passages in scripture.
'Seek ye the Lord, all ye meek of the earth... seek righteousness, seek meekness' — a call to humble repentance.
'The Lord thy God in the midst of thee is mighty; he will save, he will rejoice over thee with joy; he will rest in his love, he will joy over thee with singing.'
